title: API system service High CPU when following logs and container exits
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Running the new REST-based system service, if you call the `container/[name]/logs` endpoint, with `follow=true`, and that container stops/exits, then the CPU usage of the system service hits 100.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman system service -t 0 tcp:localhost:9944`

2. `podman run --rm -d --name foo fedora sleep 100d`

3. `curl --output - "http://localhost:9944/v1/libpod/containers/foo/logs?stdout=true&stderr=true&follow=true"`

4. `podman kill foo`

**Describe the results you received:**

CPU usage of system service process pegs at 100%

**Describe the results you expected:**

normal CPU usage. perhaps even the server closing the http connection to anyone listening on `/logs` when the container exits, but that's perhaps a new issue.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Same behavior when running a command that will respond to whatever signal `podman stop` sends, and the container exits via `podman stop foo`. In my example, with `sleep`, the process does not respond to `podman stop` and will get killed after a few seconds. But when using a responsive program, will still get CPU bug with `podman stop`.


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.2
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.4
Git Commit:   201c9505b88f451ca877d29a73ed0f1836bb96c7
Built:        Sun Jul 12 16:46:58 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
  swapFree: 0

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```

comments.body: @vrothberg did you recent PR catch this?
I just pulled and built master. Can confirm the bug does not happen anymore. Looks like this can be closed by https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/6916
